examples.title: Algorithm: Append String
examples.description: Demonstrates appending a string to a mutable buffer
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AppendString(buffer, string)
INPUT: buffer (mutable string), string (string to append)
OUTPUT: buffer (updated mutable string)

BEGIN
 IF buffer is empty THEN
 buffer = string
 ELSE
 buffer = buffer + string
 END IF
 RETURN buffer
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to append a string to a mutable buffer. It checks if the buffer is empty and if so, sets the buffer to the input string. Otherwise, it concatenates the input string to the buffer.

examples.title: Java: StringBuffer Append
examples.description: Demonstrates appending a string to a StringBuffer object
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates how to append a string to a StringBuffer object. It creates a new StringBuffer object with the initial string "Hello", appends the string " World", and then prints the resulting string.

examples.title: Java: StringBuilder Append
examples.description: Demonstrates appending a string to a StringBuilder object
examples.explanation: This Java code demonstrates how to append a string to a StringBuilder object. It creates a new StringBuilder object with the initial string "Hello", appends the string " World", and then prints the resulting string.
